project_reference.core_research_question: Can the Standard Model + GR be derived from the requirement that a composite system faithfully models itself?
project_reference.current_focus: v8.0 Gap C Algebraic Closure via C*-Measurement Maps

scope.question: Can the experiential measure framework on structure space be formalized with rigorous proofs — specifically, can Theorem A (BB negligibility) be assembled from its constituent lemmas, can the experiential density rho's Lipschitz stability be proven, and does the Born-Fisher-Experiential conjecture survive a qubit falsification test?
in_scope: Assemble Theorem A proof from BEGK, Freidlin-Wentzell, Donsker-Varadhan, and QSD lemmas with explicit error scaling
Prove Lipschitz stability of rho(P) under kernel perturbations
Compute I_vN(B;M) and S_vN(B) for toy qubit model to test Born-Fisher conjecture
out_of_scope: Countable state space generalization (Stage 1 of extension program)
Full variational structure of mu_Q beyond qubit test
Reference measure nu on structure space
Eternal inflation regime (fixed epsilon, T -> infinity)
unresolved_questions: Exact form of L(|Omega|, spectral_gap) in Lipschitz bound
Whether qubit model needs 2x2 or larger Hilbert space to be informative

user_asserted_anchors: Toy model rho = 0.347 for self-modeling observer
Parametric peak at tracking accuracy alpha ~= 0.498 with I*/H = 0.507
Three-state chain verification matches analytical formula within 1% error
known_good_baselines: Seven 16-state Markov chain toy model results from composite_self_model.py
Three-state Freidlin-Wentzell metastability verification from three_state_chain.py
context_gaps: Explicit constants in BEGK lemma composition not yet worked through
Qubit model Hilbert space dimension not yet decided
crucial_inputs: Theorem A 7-lemma dependency graph from draft Section 8
Experiential density definition rho(p) = I(B;M) * (1 - I(B;M)/H(B))

formulations: Finite irreducible continuous-time Markov chains with reversible Metropolis-type dynamics
Product space Omega = B x M with factorization condition on transition kernel
allowed_estimator_families: direct proof composition from established lemmas
forbidden_estimator_families: proof by analogy without explicit bounds
stop_and_rethink_conditions: BEGK/DV/QSD error bounds do not compose — exponential suppression breaks in the full chain
I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 fails for Born-rule distributions in qubit model

observables.name: Theorem A proof completeness
observables.definition: Self-contained proof assembling BEGK, FW, DV, QSD lemmas showing mu_BB/mu_stable <= C*exp(-(Delta_s - Delta_b - alpha)/epsilon)

observables.name: Lipschitz constant characterization
observables.definition: Proven bound |rho(P) - rho(P')| <= L*||P-P'||_inf with L depending on |Omega| and spectral gap

observables.name: Qubit Born-Fisher test result
observables.definition: Numerical value of I_vN(B;M)/S_vN(B) for Born-rule distributions in toy qubit composite process

claims.statement: Theorem A can be assembled as a complete proof from established metastability lemmas with explicit error scaling
The experiential density rho is Lipschitz continuous in the transition kernel with characterizable constant
Born-rule distributions satisfy or violate I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 in the toy qubit model

deliverables.description: Self-contained proof of Theorem A with 7 lemmas composed, explicit error scaling, constants deferred to appendix
Proof of Lipschitz stability of rho with L characterized in terms of |Omega| and spectral gap
Python implementation of toy qubit composite process computing I_vN(B;M) and S_vN(B) for Born-rule and non-Born distributions

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-theorem-a
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify each lemma cites the specific theorem from the source literature, verify the composition chain has no gaps, verify the final bound has the claimed exponential form
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Complete logical chain from lemma statements to Theorem A conclusion with no deferred steps in the main argument

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-lipschitz
acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify perturbation theory for stationary distributions is applied correctly, verify MI continuity bound is tight, verify L dependence is explicit
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Rigorous bound with no hand-waving steps; numerical verification against toy model perturbations

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-born-fisher
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run qubit model code, compute I_vN(B;M)/S_vN(B) for Born-rule distribution, compare to 0.5
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Clear numerical answer: ratio equals 0.5 (within numerical precision) or demonstrably does not

references.locator: Bovier, Eckhoff, Gayrard, Klein — Metastability papers (2001-2004)
references.why_it_matters: Provides exponential residence time bounds used in Theorem A Lemma 2

references.locator: Freidlin, Wentzell — Random Perturbations of Dynamical Systems (1984/2012)
references.why_it_matters: Provides cycle hierarchy and communication heights for basin decomposition in Theorem A Lemma 1

references.locator: Donsker, Varadhan — Large deviations for Markov processes (1975-1983)
references.why_it_matters: Provides concentration inequality for weighted empirical measure in Theorem A Lemma 6

references.locator: Baez, Dolan — From Finite Sets to Feynman Diagrams (2001)
references.why_it_matters: Defines groupoid cardinality that motivates the 1/|Aut(x)| weighting on structure space

references.locator: Lopez-Ruiz, Mancini, Calbet — A statistical measure of complexity (1995)
references.why_it_matters: The rho density belongs to this family — establishes the pedigree of the I*(1-I/H) functional form
Places rho in the broader family of measures that peak between order and randomness

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-theorem-a
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Another proof sketch with 'the tools exist' language but no assembled composition
forbidden_proxies.reason: This is exactly what the existing draft already has — repeating it is not progress

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-lipschitz
forbidden_proxies.proxy: More toy model numerical runs confirming intuition without formal Lipschitz bound
forbidden_proxies.reason: Numerical evidence for rho stability already exists in the parametric sweep; the gap is the proof

weakest_anchors: BEGK error bounds may not compose cleanly across all 7 lemmas — the individual bounds are established but their product has not been verified to preserve the exponential form
The qubit model dimension choice may be too small to be informative about the general conjecture
unvalidated_assumptions: Reversibility assumption in Theorem A (real observers are non-equilibrium)
Factorization condition exactness (real systems have approximate factorization)
competing_explanations: Alternative density functions in the LMC family with same boundary conditions but different peak behavior could work equally well
disconfirming_observations: BEGK/DV/QSD error bounds do not compose — exponential suppression of mu_BB/mu_stable breaks in the assembled proof
Born-rule distributions do NOT satisfy I_vN(B;M) = S_vN(B)/2 in the qubit model

position.current_phase_name: Integration and Gap C Resolution Statement
position.status: v8.0 milestone complete (2026-03-29). Phase 31 plans skipped -- they would have downgraded Paper 7's correct complexification claim by conflating basin-only impossibility with observer+basin impossibility.
position.last_activity_desc: v8.0 milestone complete. Phases 28-30 established: observable algebra = M_16(R), three impossibility theorems for basin-only forcing, selection argument formalized. Paper 7 complexification claim (C*-observer forces it) remains correct.

active_calculations: ALGV-01: L_{E_{11}} = (1/2)*I_{16} on V_{1/2} -- COMPLETE (zero error)
ALGV-02: V_0 channel J^2=-Id search -- COMPLETE (negative, structural impossibility)
ALGV-03: Associative closure of {T_b} on V_{1/2} -- COMPLETE (= M_16(R), 256-dim)
REPR-01: J_u Clifford characterization -- COMPLETE (grade 2+3, NOT 10th generator)
REPR-02: J_u algebraic forcing verdict -- COMPLETE (distinguished but not forced; containment vacuous)
IMPL-01: Impossibility theorems -- COMPLETE (3 theorems proved, 17 tests pass)
IMPL-02: Selection argument -- COMPLETE (5-link chain, Gap C = selection-conditional)
intermediate_results: **ALGV-01**: L_{E_{11}} = (1/2)*I_{16} on V_{1/2} (EXACT, zero error) -- numerical confirmation of v6.0 Phase 22 Route 1 (HIGH)
**ALGV-02**: V_0 channel NEGATIVE -- all 10 T_b are symmetric, J_u is antisymmetric, residual = 4.0 (HIGH)
**T_b Clifford structure**: 9 traceless T_b satisfy {T_a,T_b} = (1/2)*delta_{ab}*I -- they ARE Cl(9) generators on V_{1/2} (HIGH)
**Key insight**: J_u lies in associative closure but NOT in linear span of T_b (HIGH)
open_questions: ~~Does any element of the operator algebra generated by {T_b : b in V_0 = h_2(O)} on V_{1/2} satisfy J^2 = -Id?~~ ANSWERED: NO (structural impossibility -- T_b symmetric, J needs antisymmetric)

~~Is the full observable algebra on V_{1/2} (all Peirce multiplication COMPOSITIONS) large enough to contain J_u?~~ ANSWERED: YES trivially (closure = M_16(R) = all 16x16 matrices), but containment is vacuous
~~Does V_0 = h_2(O), being a JC-algebra, provide richer structure transmission than V_1 = R?~~ ANSWERED: Yes (10-dim vs 1-dim), but still insufficient for J (wrong symmetry class)
~~If algebraic forcing fails, is "no chirality -> no self-modelers" rigorous enough for a theorem?~~ ANSWERED: No -- it's "argued, not proved" (L4 weakest link). Gap C = selection-conditional.

~~What is the physical meaning of needing operator compositions (iterated measurements) rather than single Peirce multiplication to reach J_u?~~ ANSWERED: J_u enters at depth 2 (compositions of 3 Peirce operators), but this is vacuous since closure is everything
NEW: Why does the Peirce-derived spin(9) differ from Krasnov's spin(9)? What is the physical significance of having two distinct spin(9) subalgebras of M_16(R)?
NEW: Can the choice of u in S^6 be derived from the self-modeling framework, or is it necessarily external input?
NEW: Is the reduced stabilizer (dim 10 = su(3)+u(1)^2) or Krasnov's (dim 12 = su(3)+su(2)+u(1)) the physically correct one?

decisions.summary: Three impossibility theorems proved -- Schur commutant dim=1, J_u grade-3 separation, u in S^6 = Gap B2. 71 tests pass.
Gap C = algebraic impossibility (theorem) + selection-conditional (argued). L4 = weakest link.
REPR-02 verdict -- J_u is distinguished (isolated, grade 2+3) but NOT a 10th Clifford generator. Spin(10) extension fails. Gap C cannot close purely algebraically.
ALGV-03 confirmed -- associative closure = M_16(R) (256-dim). Clifford rescaling: gamma_1 = 4*T_b[1], gamma_k = 2*T_b[k] for k=2..9.
Krasnov discrepancy resolved -- Peirce spin(9) != Krasnov spin(9) in M_16(R). Both give correct stabilizer dims for their embeddings.
ALGV-01 V_1=R bottleneck numerically confirmed with zero error
ALGV-02 V_0 channel NEGATIVE -- T_b operators symmetric, J_u antisymmetric
Started milestone v9.0: Continuum Limit from Finite-Dimensional Observer
decisions.rationale: New milestone cycle: close all four Paper 6 gaps via finite-dim observer as UV cutoff

custom_conventions.complex_structure: u = e_7 by default (any u in S^6 equivalent under G_2)

blockers: ~~V_0 channel is genuinely unexplored~~ RESOLVED: V_0 channel fully characterized in Phase 28-29
~~Observable algebra dimension unknown~~ RESOLVED: closure = M_16(R) = 256-dim (full matrix algebra)
~~REPR-02 confirmed Path A (algebraic forcing fails): Phase 30 must formalize impossibility + selection argument~~ RESOLVED: Phase 30 complete -- 3 theorems + selection argument
Two distinct spin(9) embeddings in M_16(R) -- physical significance unclear, noted in Krasnov discrepancy
Phase 31 (Integration) must update Paper 7 gap register with Phase 30 results
Krasnov stabilizer dim discrepancy (10 vs 12) needs interpretation: which spin(9) is physically relevant?
